Nach einem absurden Busunfall startet für den Büro-Sklaven Arihito Atobe ein neues Leben in einer Fantasywelt. Als sogenannter Seeker landet er in der seltenen Klasse „Nachthut“, die die Party aus der hinteren Reihe mit Angriff, Verteidigung und Heilung unterstützt. Seine Gruppe besteht nur aus Mädchen, und seine Klasse hat einen skurrilen Bonus: Allein hinten zu stehen steigert ihre Zuneigung zu ihm. All LLMs (chatgpt and the like) are all A.I. systems.  But not all A.I. systems are LLMs.  A.I. is about 70 years old.  Modern LLMs are about a decade old.

LLMs can fill the gaps in your knowledge, in real time, as you communicate with them.  They can find patterns that you would never have found on your own.  LLMs can check hypothesizes you may have and you can get a better picture of the consequences and contradictions.2/3

A.I. is a tool.  It serves a certain function when used properly. From my experience it amplifies my abilities to use my computer science education.  I believe this would be true for anyone who uses A.I. for a familiar subject matter.  LLMs are not search engines. 1/3

record.text: Tell her you don’t take nutritional advice from people who ate lead paint as kids… and doubly so when they are still eating them as an adult.
Is this how Microsoft came up with the “this is an Xbox” campaign…? By just feeding Copilot pictures of random tech with an Xbox in the corner to see what it said?

You’re not “stuck”, this is just the friction before you lift 170lbs for the first time. 

As long as you keep working, you WILL lift 170… then 180, then 190, then… well, who knows where your ceiling is? You’re going to do it!

No one says you can't still solve problems with your own brain, you just need to be conversant with the current tools of the job which, for now, includes Claude/Copilot/etc. My employer is not mandating AI yet, but my mentor's Fortune 500 company is.
To use AI by his work and has found it useful in coding, but he's mostly doing just enough to be seen to be using it.

I'm just getting started in this, but I think you'll (we'll) need to demonstrate competence using AI to assist with coding at least in the near future. My upline is encouraging me to use Claude but I haven't started yet. And my mentor, also very much an AI skeptic, has been required...

Maybe instead of "Copilot", indicating a trusted, trained, professional colleague, they should have named it "Overconfident Passenger" and let you lock the cabin door to keep it back there where he couldn't mess with anything important?

I keep imagining Microsoft running late night TV ads for Copilot like Miss Cleo.
Ah well. We should jam sometime, I miss getting overrun by Zombies.
